channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Southern areas hit by winter storm thaw and power slowly returns
item.description: After a freezing winter storm shut schools, cut power and cancelled or delayed flights, the South was slowly thawing Sunday as weather warmed.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Reality TV star Chase Chrisley accused of slapping Twin Peaks bar manager in Atlanta
item.description: Chrisley, who gained fame on the “Chrisley Knows Best” show that featured his parents and close-knit family, was allegedly drunk at a Twin Peaks restaurant on Jan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden, Netanyahu discuss latest in effort to reach an Israel-Hamas war ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and U.S. President Joe Biden spoke Sunday about efforts to reach a ceasefire and hostage release deal in the Israel-Hamas war, a sign of the intensifying push to reach a deal before Donald Trump’s inauguration next week.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hurricane Katrina victim forced to evacuate again, this time for Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The California wildfires are hitting too close to home for a woman who used to live in New Orleans. She was forced to evacuate during Hurricane Katrina, and now she's evacuating once again.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, KRON4

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: “It’s horrible when you realize that you’re the one that actually gave them the milk that killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Los Angeles tree advocates hope their vital work recovers from devastating fires
item.description: Some people in Los Angeles have worked for years to increase the number of trees that provide shade and respite from heat and air pollution.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber And Dorany Pineda, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jackson and Henry lead the way as the Ravens run over the rival Steelers 28-14 in the AFC playoffs
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson handed off to Derrick Henry, then slid out to the left as if he still had the ball. A couple linebackers shifted into Jackson's path, leaving the middle open for Henry to run through for a 44-yard touchdown.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles County medical examiner's office confirms wildfire death toll rises to 16
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County medical examiner’s office confirmed the death toll from the wildfires ravaging the area has risen to 16. The total of confirmed fatalities stands at 16 victims, and the cases remain under investigation.
item.dc:creator: JAE HONG, HOLLY RAMER and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Everything you need to know about the water drops in California during wildfires
item.description: Firefighters in California are battling the fires from the sky using a variety of specialized aircraft that dump water and fire retardant onto the terrain below.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Are there no Michelin-starred restaurants near you? There's a reason for that
item.description: You'd have difficulty finding Michelin-starred restaurants in most states — but it's not because there aren't excellent eateries in those places.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, spoke with the Pope by phone on Saturday to tell him he was a recipient of the medal, which is the highest civilian honor.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Powerful winter storm that dumped snow in US South maintains its icy grip
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Flight cancellations piled up and officials warned of continuing dangerous roads Saturday in the wake of a winter storm that brought biting cold and wet snow to the U.S. South, leading to school closures and disrupting travel.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LA's fire chief is at the center of a public spat with City Hall as wildfires rage
item.description: Kristin Crowley was elevated to Los Angeles fire chief in 2022 at a time of turmoil in a department consumed by complaints of rampant hazing, harassment and discrimination among its 3,400-member ranks. As a career firefighter, she was portrayed by the then-mayor as a stabilizing force.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart recalls 12,000 cartons of chicken broth
item.description: Walmart has recalled over 12,000 cartons of Great Value Family Size Chicken Broth due to packaging issues that could lead to spoilage, according to the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gun jams as woman tries to shoot boyfriend: Tennessee police
item.description: A Tennessee woman is facing an attempted murder charge after reportedly attempting to shoot her boyfriend before her gun jammed.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, NewsNation

item.title: Winter weather road tips: Staying safe while navigating the snow and ice
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Snowfall brings a whole bundle of headaches for drivers, but there are some steps that can be taken to avoid lingering issues or a potentially dangerous situation.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KRON4

item.title: LA fire chief says city failed department amid ongoing Southern California wildfires
item.description: Los Angeles Fire Department (LAFD) Chief Kristin Crowley said during an interview Friday that the city failed the department amid the ongoing wildfires in Southern California that have taken at least 11 lives and burned more than 37,000 acres.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: MAP: Emergency room visits for respiratory illnesses are soaring in these states
item.description: A number of states are seeing a spike in hospital visits caused by respiratory illnesses as we enter the New Year.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Pence said he congratulated Trump on his win at Carter’s funeral
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said he congratulated President-elect Trump on his upcoming return to the White House when the two shared a brief greeting at former President Jimmy Carter’s funeral service on Thursday. “He greeted me when he came down the aisle. I stood up, extended my hand.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FEMA kicks hurricane survivors out of temporary housing into snowstorm and freezing temperatures
item.description: Approximately 2,000 North Carolina households that survived Hurricane Helene will have to move out of their FEMA-expensed hotel rooms on Saturday amid a winter storm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lisa Kudrow says Matthew Perry left a note for her inside a recognizable 'Friends' prop
item.description: "I hadn't opened it up or looked inside it, but yeah he did. He had a note in there and I forgot about it," Kudrow said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold law that would force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday seemed likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg returns to his wildfire-charred LA neighborhood
item.description: Steve Guttenberg awoke Thursday morning to a grim reality: The treacherous wildfire that tore through the Pacific Palisades had left his once-lush neighborhood charred and unrecognizable.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LIVE: Fires devastating LA grow more slowly as winds die down
item.description: The two biggest fires devastating the Los Angeles area grew just slightly Friday as firefighters beat back the blazes that have killed at least 10 people, obliterated neighborhoods and left the nation's second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: LA area's 2 biggest blazes burn at least 10,000 structures, while new fire leads to more evacuations
item.description: The two biggest wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area have killed at least 10 people and burned more than 10,000 homes and other structures, officials said as they urged more people to heed evacuation orders after a new blaze ignited and quickly grew.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Anita Bryant, anti-gay rights activist and singer, dead at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant Dry, known popularly Anita Bryant — a singer and crusader against the progress of gay rights — has died at the age of 84, her family announced in an obituary.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Far from wildfire flames, residents of sunny Los Angeles go about their lives in disquiet and fear
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Pedestrians shuffled by the famed Chateau Marmont hotel, customers queued up at Starbucks on Sunset Boulevard and car horns bleated at gridlocked intersections.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As wildfires burn in LA, what to know about protective masks
item.description: Certain masks are effective in protecting against wildfire smoke — but only if they are fitted and worn properly.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Jean Smart calls on TV networks to not air award shows, instead donate to Los Angeles wildfire relief
item.description: Jean Smart is urging TV networks to refrain from airing glitzy awards shows amid the devastating wildfires in the Los Angeles area, and to donate money to relief efforts instead.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: All 5 living presidents, political dignitaries attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Political leaders gathered to honor the life of former President Jimmy Carter during his state funeral at the Washington National Cathedral on Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Firefighters battle devastating Los Angeles wildfires as winds calm somewhat
item.description: EDITOR’S NOTE: Click HERE to view The Associated Press’ wildfire tracker, which shows where wildfires are spreading.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: LIVE: More evacuations ordered after fast-moving Kenneth fire ignites in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters are racing to contain a new wildfire that broke out close to homes in the area of Calabasas and Hidden Hills, northwest of Los Angeles, on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5

item.title: Harrowing escapes from Los Angeles wildfires were made by foot, by car or by the grace of strangers
item.description: EDITOR'S NOTE: Click HERE to view The Associated Press' wildfire tracker, which shows where wildfires are spreading. PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Flames and pillars of smoke rose from both sides of the road and a woman yelled in panic as firefighters ushered a crowd of fleeing residents along.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, BEATRICE DUPUY, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, GENE JOHNSON and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, WGN 9
